matched_rules.text: Grievance: Any dispute a tenant may have with respect to HACC action or failure to
If HUD has issued a due process determination, HACC may exclude from HACC grievance
In states without due process determinations, HACC must grant opportunity for grievance

matched_entities.name: Grievance: Any dispute a tenant may have with respect to HACC action or failure to
If HUD has issued a due process determination, HACC may exclude from HACC grievance
In states without due process determinations, HACC must grant opportunity for grievance
The notice must also state that the tenant may request a grievance hearing on the HACC’s
adverse action and will provide the subject of the record and the tenant a copy of such

Grievance: Any dispute a tenant may have with respect to HACC action or failure to", "key_facts": { "incident_summary": "Retaliation complaint anchored to HACC core housing policies", "evidence_query": "retaliation grievance complaint appeal hearing due process tenant policy adverse action", "evidence_summary": "The strongest supporting material is 'ADMISSIONS AND CONTINUED OCCUPANCY POLICY'.

I am trying to follow HACC’s policies and use the grievance process, but I feel shut down and threatened for doing so.

Key facts:
- I raised a complaint about my housing situation and requested a grievance.
- Soon after, HACC took adverse actions against me.
- I asked for an appeal or hearing and did not feel due process was provided.
- The retaliation has impacted my housing security and wellbeing.

I am asking HACC to stop the retaliatory actions, provide a fair grievance hearing, and follow its own policies regarding tenant disputes and due process.

Grievance: Any dispute a tenant may have with respect to HACC action or failure to", "key_facts": { "incident_summary": "Retaliation complaint anchored to HACC core housing policies", "evidence_query": "retaliation grievance complaint appeal hearing due process tenant policy adverse action", "evidence_summary": "The strongest supporting material is 'ADMISSIONS AND CONTINUED OCCUPANCY POLICY'.

I am trying to follow HACC’s policies and use the grievance process, but I feel shut down and threatened for doing so.

Key facts:
- I raised a complaint about my housing situation and requested a grievance.
- Soon after, HACC took adverse actions against me.
- I asked for an appeal or hearing and did not feel due process was provided.
- The retaliation has impacted my housing security and wellbeing.

I am asking HACC to stop the retaliatory actions, provide a fair grievance hearing, and follow its own policies regarding tenant disputes and due process.

Policy: Nondiscrimination
Policy Number: 3364 -50-02
Approving Officer: President
Effective date: December 21, 2022
Responsible Agent: Equal Opportunity
Coordinator Original effective date: December 1, 2007
Scope: All University of Toledo Campuses
New policy proposal Minor/technical revision of existing policy
Major revision of existing policy Reaffirmation of existing policy
(A) Policy statement
The University of Toledo does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, age,
national origin, ancestry, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, military or veteran
status, the presence of a disability, genetic information, familial status, political affiliation, or
participation in protected activities in its provision of employment and educational opportunities.
Discrimination, including discriminatory harassment, on any of those bases is strictly prohibited.
Upon notice of possible discrimination, the University takes prompt and appropriate steps to
determine what occurred, end a discriminatory practice or hostile environment if one has been
created, and prevent its recurrence.

"initial_complaint_text": "Complaint:\n\nI am filing this retaliation complaint about how HACC handled my situation after I raised concerns and tried to use the grievance process.

about how HACC handled my situation after I raised concerns and tried to use the grievance process. I made a complaint about my housing conditions and asked for help, and shortly after that I started receiving adverse actions from HACC. It feels like I was targeted because I spoke up.

What happened, in my own words: I contacted HACC to report issues in my unit and asked for a grievance because I disagreed with HACC’s action and their failure to fix the problem. After that, I received notices and decisions that seemed unrelated to my original issue and were harmful to me. The timing and pattern make it feel like retaliation. I asked for a fair grievance hearing and due process, but I did not feel I was given a meaningful chance to explain my side or appeal before the action was taken.

This has affected me a lot. It has caused stress, fear about my housing stability, and a sense that I can’t safely raise concerns without being punished.

r about my housing stability, and a sense that I can’t safely raise concerns without being punished. I am trying to follow HACC’s policies and use the grievance process, but I feel shut down and threatened for doing so.

Key facts:
- I raised a complaint about my housing situation and requested a grievance.
- Soon after, HACC took adverse actions against me.
- I asked for an appeal or hearing and did not feel due process was provided.
- The retaliation has impacted my housing security and wellbeing.

I am asking HACC to stop the retaliatory actions, provide a fair grievance hearing, and follow its own policies regarding tenant disputes and due process. I want a clear explanation of the actions taken against me and a chance to be heard without fear of further retaliation.",

entities.name: I am trying to follow HACC’s policies and use the grievance process, but I feel shut down and threatened for doing so.\n

attributes.text:
I am trying to follow HACC’s policies and use the grievance process, but I feel shut down and threatened for doing so.

Key facts:
- I raised a complaint about my housing situation and requested a grievance.
- Soon after, HACC took adverse actions against me.
- I asked for an appeal or hearing and did not feel due process was provided.
- The retaliation has impacted my housing security and wellbeing.

I am asking HACC to stop the retaliatory actions, provide a fair grievance hearing, and follow its own policies regarding tenant disputes and due process.

entities.name: I want a clear explanation of the actions taken against me and a chance to be heard without fear of further retaliation.

- Soon after, HACC took adverse actions against me.
- I asked for an appeal or hearing and did not feel due process was provided.
- The retaliation has impacted my housing security and wellbeing.

I am asking HACC to stop the retaliatory actions, provide a fair grievance hearing, and follow its own policies regarding tenant disputes and due process.

I am trying to follow HACC’s policies and use the grievance process, but I feel shut down and threatened for doing so.

Key facts:
- I raised a complaint about my housing situation and requested a grievance.
- Soon after, HACC took adverse actions against me.
- I asked for an appeal or hearing and did not feel due process was provided.
- The retaliation has impacted my housing security and wellbeing.

I am asking HACC to stop the retaliatory actions, provide a fair grievance hearing, and follow its own policies regarding tenant disputes and due process.

"initial_complaint_text": "Complaint:\n\nI am filing this retaliation complaint about how HACC handled my situation after I raised concerns and tried to use the grievance process. I made a complaint about my housing conditions and asked for help, and shortly after that I started receiving adverse actions from HACC.

conditions and asked for help, and shortly after that I started receiving adverse actions from HACC. It feels like I was targeted because I spoke up.

What happened, in my own words: I contacted HACC to report issues in my unit and asked for a grievance because I disagreed with HACC’s action and their failure to fix the problem. After that, I received notices and decisions that seemed unrelated to my original issue and were harmful to me. The timing and pattern make it feel like retaliation. I asked for a fair grievance hearing and due process, but I did not feel I was given a meaningful chance to explain my side or appeal before the action was taken.

This has affected me a lot. It has caused stress, fear about my housing stability, and a sense that I can’t safely raise concerns without being punished.

entities.name: I am trying to follow HACC\u2019s policies and use the grievance process, but I feel shut down and threatened for doing

attributes.text:
I am trying to follow HACC’s policies and use the grievance process, but I feel shut down and threatened for doing so.

Key facts:
- I raised a complaint about my housing situation and requested a grievance.
- Soon after, HACC took adverse actions against me.
- I asked for an appeal or hearing and did not feel due process was provided.
- The retaliation has impacted my housing security and wellbeing.

I am asking HACC to stop the retaliatory actions, provide a fair grievance hearing, and follow its own policies regarding tenant disputes and due process.

facts.text:
I am trying to follow HACC’s policies and use the grievance process, but I feel shut down and threatened for doing so.

Key facts:
- I raised a complaint about my housing situation and requested a grievance.
- Soon after, HACC took adverse actions against me.
- I asked for an appeal or hearing and did not feel due process was provided.
- The retaliation has impacted my housing security and wellbeing.

I am asking HACC to stop the retaliatory actions, provide a fair grievance hearing, and follow its own policies regarding tenant disputes and due process.

metadata.text:
I am trying to follow HACC’s policies and use the grievance process, but I feel shut down and threatened for doing so.

Key facts:
- I raised a complaint about my housing situation and requested a grievance.
- Soon after, HACC took adverse actions against me.
- I asked for an appeal or hearing and did not feel due process was provided.
- The retaliation has impacted my housing security and wellbeing.

I am asking HACC to stop the retaliatory actions, provide a fair grievance hearing, and follow its own policies regarding tenant disputes and due process.

facts.text: \n\nI\u2019ve tried to use the grievance process as HACC\u2019s policies describe \u2014 a grievance is a dispute about HACC\u2019s action or failure to act \u2014 but I didn\u2019t feel I got a fair chance to explain my side before decisions were made.

metadata.text: \n\nI\u2019ve tried to use the grievance process as HACC\u2019s policies describe \u2014 a grievance is a dispute about HACC\u2019s action or failure to act \u2014 but I didn\u2019t feel I got a fair chance to explain my side before decisions were made.

facts.text: After that, I received notices and decisions that seemed unrelated to my original issue and were harmful to me.

facts.text: I asked for a fair grievance hearing and due process, but I did not feel I was given a meaningful chance to explain my side or appeal before the action was taken.\n\nThis has affected me a lot.

metadata.text: I asked for a fair grievance hearing and due process, but I did not feel I was given a meaningful chance to explain my side or appeal before the action was taken.\n\nThis has affected me a lot.

facts.text: It has caused stress, fear about my housing stability, and a sense that I can\u2019t safely raise concerns without being punished.

metadata.text: It has caused stress, fear about my housing stability, and a sense that I can\u2019t safely raise concerns without being punished.

facts.text: The timing and pattern make it feel like retaliation.

metadata.text: The timing and pattern make it feel like retaliation.

facts.text: It feels like I was targeted because I spoke up.\n\nWhat happened, in my own words: I contacted HACC to report issues in my unit and asked for a grievance because I disagreed with HACC\u2019s action and their failure to fix the problem.

metadata.text: It feels like I was targeted because I spoke up.\n\nWhat happened, in my own words: I contacted HACC to report issues in my unit and asked for a grievance because I disagreed with HACC\u2019s action and their failure to fix the problem.

facts.text: Practically, the adverse actions and notices have made me feel unstable and distracted, and it\u2019s been harder to focus on daily life because I\u2019m constantly worried about what comes next.

metadata.text: I want a clear explanation of the actions taken against me and a chance to be heard without fear of further retaliation.", "conversation_history": [ { "role": "complainant", "type": "initial_complaint", "content": "Complaint:\n\nI am filing this retaliation complaint about how HACC handled my situation after I raised concerns and tried to use the grievance process.

facts.text:
I am trying to follow HACC’s policies and use the grievance process, but I feel shut down and threatened for doing so.

Key facts:
- I raised a complaint about my housing situation and requested a grievance.
- Soon after, HACC took adverse actions against me.
- I asked for an appeal or hearing and did not feel due process was provided.
- The retaliation has impacted my housing security and wellbeing.

I am asking HACC to stop the retaliatory actions, provide a fair grievance hearing, and follow its own policies regarding tenant disputes and due process.

I am asking HACC to stop the retaliatory actions, provide a fair grievance hearing, and follow its own policies regarding tenant disputes and due process.

"seed_source": "hacc_research_engine", "anchor_sections": [ "general_policy" ] } }, "initial_complaint_text": "Complaint:\n\nI am filing this retaliation complaint about how HACC handled my situation after I raised concerns and tried to use the grievance process.

facts.text: It has caused stress, fear about my housing stability, and a sense that I can’t safely raise concerns without being punished.

metadata.text: It has caused stress, fear about my housing stability, and a sense that I can’t safely raise concerns without being punished.

facts.text: I made a complaint about my housing conditions and asked for help, and shortly after that I started receiving adverse actions from HACC.

metadata.text: I made a complaint about my housing conditions and asked for help, and shortly after that I started receiving adverse actions from HACC.

facts.text: Practically, the adverse actions and notices have made me feel unstable and distracted, and it’s been harder to focus on daily life because I’m constantly worried about what comes next.

metadata.text: Practically, the adverse actions and notices have made me feel unstable and distracted, and it’s been harder to focus on daily life because I’m constantly worried about what comes next.

facts.text:
I am trying to follow HACC’s policies and use the grievance process, but I feel shut down and threatened for doing so.

Key facts:
- I raised a complaint about my housing situation and requested a grievance.
- Soon after, HACC took adverse actions against me.
- I asked for an appeal or hearing and did not feel due process was provided.
- The retaliation has impacted my housing security and wellbeing.

I am asking HACC to stop the retaliatory actions, provide a fair grievance hearing, and follow its own policies regarding tenant disputes and due process.

metadata.text:

- Soon after, HACC took adverse actions against me.
- I asked for an appeal or hearing and did not feel due process was provided.
- The retaliation has impacted my housing security and wellbeing.

I am asking HACC to stop the retaliatory actions, provide a fair grievance hearing, and follow its own policies regarding tenant disputes and due process.

facts.text: Emotionally, I’ve been stressed and anxious because it feels like speaking up made things worse, and I’m worried about losing my housing.

metadata.text: Emotionally, I’ve been stressed and anxious because it feels like speaking up made things worse, and I’m worried about losing my housing.

facts.text:
I am trying to follow HACC’s policies and use the grievance process, but I feel shut down and threatened for doing so.

Key facts:
- I raised a complaint about my housing situation and requested a grievance.
- Soon after, HACC took adverse actions against me.
- I asked for an appeal or hearing and did not feel due process was provided.
- The retaliation has impacted my housing security and wellbeing.

I am asking HACC to stop the retaliatory actions, provide a fair grievance hearing, and follow its own policies regarding tenant disputes and due process.

- The retaliation has impacted my housing security and wellbeing.

I am asking HACC to stop the retaliatory actions, provide a fair grievance hearing, and follow its own policies regarding tenant disputes and due process.

facts.text: The impact has been mostly emotional and practical.

facts.text: I made a complaint about my housing conditions and asked for help, and shortly after that I started receiving adverse actions from HACC.

facts.text: The timing and pattern make it feel like retaliation.

facts.text: It has caused stress, fear about my housing stability, and a sense that I can’t safely raise concerns without being punished.

facts.text: It feels like I was targeted because I spoke up.\n\nWhat happened, in my own words: I contacted HACC to report issues in my unit and asked for a grievance because I disagreed with HACC’s action and their failure to fix the problem.

metadata.text: It feels like I was targeted because I spoke up.\n\nWhat happened, in my own words: I contacted HACC to report issues in my unit and asked for a grievance because I disagreed with HACC’s action and their failure to fix the problem.

facts.text: Practically, the adverse actions and notices have made me feel unstable and distracted, and it’s been harder to focus on daily life because I’m constantly worried about what comes next.

metadata.text: Practically, the adverse actions and notices have made me feel unstable and distracted, and it’s been harder to focus on daily life because I’m constantly worried about what comes next.

facts.text:
I am trying to follow HACC’s policies and use the grievance process, but I feel shut down and threatened for doing so.

Key facts:
- I raised a complaint about my housing situation and requested a grievance.
- Soon after, HACC took adverse actions against me.
- I asked for an appeal or hearing and did not feel due process was provided.
- The retaliation has impacted my housing security and wellbeing.

I am asking HACC to stop the retaliatory actions, provide a fair grievance hearing, and follow its own policies regarding tenant disputes and due process.
